body.title: Write theigors/rules/design-principles palace node
body.result: closed — pe_chain coding mechanism decommissioned; ticket no longer relevant to reasoning-first direction
body.description:
No design-principles node exists in theigors/rules/. The Hermes vs. OpenClaw ranking data (2026-05-10: Hermes overtook OpenClaw by optimizing for depth of self-improvement rather than breadth of platform coverage) is strong evidence for a principle worth formalizing: prefer deepening existing capabilities over adding new ones. Without a palace node, this principle gets relitigated at each design session.

Write palace node at path=theigors/rules/design-principles containing: (1) depth-over-breadth principle with Hermes citation, (2) definition and when to apply, (3) example: Hermes skill compounding vs. OpenClaw 50-channel breadth.

**Affected files:** palace DB (insert via memory_store); lab/design_docs/decisions/ echo file
**Design rules:** palace-first (palace is canonical for rules, not CLAUDE.md)
**Scope boundary:** In: single principle node. Out: other design principles (add as separate nodes when they emerge).
**Test plan:** memory_get(path='theigors/rules/design-principles') returns non-empty content. Node appears in theigors/rules/ listing.
